title: Manage Relationships in Your Atlas Entity-Relationship Diagram

sections.heading: Before You Add Relationships
sections.body:
To add relationships, ensure you have already
[Data Model Visualization](atlas-ui-data-modeling).

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
To learn how to add relationships to your entity-relationship diagram, select
the tab corresponding to your preferred method:

**Side-panel:**

Side Panel

sections.heading: Step 1: Open your .
sections.body:
From the Diagram **Data Modeling** tab, open your
entity-relationship diagram.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click .
sections.body:
In the side panel's **Relationship** tab, click
**Add Relationship**.

sections.heading: Step 3: Specify .
sections.body:
Specify values for the following fields under
Relationship Properties:

- Local collection: The primary collection in the relationship.

- Local field: The field in the primary collection.

- Local cardinality: The number of unique values in the local field.

- Foreign collection: The target collection in the relationship.

- Foreign field: The field in the target collection.

- Foreign cardinality: The number of unique values in the foreign
  field.

Atlas automatically saves and updates your changes as you
make them.

sections.heading: Step 4: Add annotations for your relationship.
sections.body:
In the side panel's **Notes** tab, you can add annotations
for your relationship. This can be helpful for documenting the
purpose and details of the relationship.

**Drag-drop:**

Drag and Drop

sections.heading: Step 1: Open your
sections.body:
From the Diagram **Data Modeling** tab, open your
entity-relationship diagram.

sections.heading: Step 2: Click the relationship  icon
sections.body:
In the upper-left corner of the diagram view, click the relationship
Relationship icon to enable drag-and-drop relationship
creation.

sections.heading: Step 3: Click one collection and drag it to another
sections.body:
When you click a collection and drag it to another,
Atlas opens the side panel and displays the
**Relationship Properties** tab.

sections.heading: Step 4: Specify .
sections.body:
Specify values for the following fields under
Relationship Properties:

- Local collection: The primary collection in the relationship.

- Local field: The field in the primary collection.

- Local cardinality: The number of unique values in the local field.

- Foreign collection: The target collection in the relationship.

- Foreign field: The field in the target collection.

- Foreign cardinality: The number of unique values in the foreign
  field.

Atlas automatically saves and updates your changes as you
make them.

sections.heading: Step 5: Add annotations for your relationship.
sections.body:
In the side panel's **Notes** tab, you can add annotations
for your relationship. This can be helpful for documenting the
purpose and details of the relationship.

After you create a relationship, Atlas displays each relationship in
the **Relationships** tab.

sections.heading: Delete Relationships
sections.body: To delete a relationship from your entity-relationship diagram:

sections.heading: Step 1: Open the relationship side panel
sections.body:
Click on the relationship in the diagram. The relationship side panel
opens on the right side of the screen.

sections.heading: Step 2: Select the relationship to delete
sections.body:
In the **Relationship Properties** tab, click the Trash
**Delete** button.
